name: Nigerian Arabic (Shuwa)
notes: Nigerian Shuwa Arabic. Spoken in Borno and Yobe states (NE Nigeria), Cameroon, Niger. Shuwa Arab ethnic group. More contact-modified than Chadian Arabic. θ → /t/, ð → /d/ (complete merger — Hausa influence). ق → /q/ educated speech; /ɡ/ vernacular. Hausa substrate: /ɓ, ɗ/ implosives; /ŋ/ phoneme. Possible incipient lexical tone in some varieties (Owens 1993 — debated). Owens (1993) *A Reference Grammar of Nigerian Arabic* is the primary source.
